steps.title: Calculate the slope using two known points
steps.content:
The line passes through $(8, 2)$ and $(0, 5)$. The slope is:
$$m = \frac{5 - 2}{0 - 8} = \frac{3}{-8} = -\frac{3}{8}$$

steps.title: Find the x-intercept using the slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Since $(0, 5)$ is the y-intercept, the equation is $y = -\frac{3}{8}x + 5$. The point $(c, 0)$ lies on this line, so substitute $y = 0$:
$$0 = -\frac{3}{8}c + 5$$
$$\frac{3}{8}c = 5$$
$$c = 5 \cdot \frac{8}{3} = \frac{40}{3}$$
Equivalent forms accepted: 40/3, 13.333.
